key_insight: HVAF spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that retain martensitic transformation and functional shape-memory behavior, provided the coatings are annealed after deposition.
engineering_relevance: This enables durable, adherent NiTi functional coatings on steel for applications needing wear resistance plus thermal actuation or self-adaptive behavior.
method: HVAF-sprayed NiTi onto mild steel using four processing parameter sets
Measured composition, porosity, microstructure, phase transformation, and thermomechanical response
Compared as-sprayed versus annealed coatings in bending, nanoindentation, and scratch tests
evidence_signal: The coatings were 100–300 µm thick, adhered well to the substrate, and showed martensitic transformation plus shape-memory/thermal actuation in mechanical tests.
watch_item: As-sprayed coatings were highly defective and chemically inhomogeneous, so post-spray annealing is necessary and tensile strength remains limited.
paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
